executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

**Enrollment and Spending Trends**

National Medicaid enrollment totaled 75.7 million beneficiaries as of December 2025, with most major states experiencing declining enrollment following the end of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. Texas maintained 4.1 million enrollees with a modest 2.2% year-over-year decline, performing better than larger states like California (-5.6%) and Illinois (-6.5%), though lagging North Carolina's 1.6% growth. Total national expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a national average per-enrollee cost of $2,851. Texas spending totaled $12.0 billion with a per-enrollee cost of $2,950, positioning the state near the national average and significantly below high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,368 per enrollee) and Ohio ($3,522 per enrollee). Notably, all major states reported substantial year-over-year spending decreases ranging from 24% to 59%, likely reflecting post-pandemic enrollment normalization and policy adjustments.

**Quality Performance and State Comparisons**

Quality measure performance reveals significant interstate variation, with national scores averaging 65.6 out of 100 points. Texas scored 62 points, ranking 30th nationally in the "moderate" performance tier, trailing quality leaders like Massachusetts (81), Minnesota (80), and Vermont (79), while outperforming lower-tier states in the South and Southwest. The state's managed care penetration rate of 93.3% significantly exceeds the national average of 83.2%, suggesting strong care coordination infrastructure that could support quality improvements. With 15 states scoring below 60 points and only 10 achieving high-performer status above 75 points, Texas has clear opportunity for advancement through targeted quality initiatives, particularly given its robust managed care platform and relatively stable enrollment base compared to peer states experiencing more dramatic membership fluctuations.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.08 million enrollees, representing substantial coverage despite not expanding Medicaid under the ACA. The state demonstrates relative enrollment stability with a -2.2% year-over-year decline, outperforming most peer states including California (-5.6%) and Illinois (-6.5%), and falling below the steeper declines seen in fellow non-expansion states like Florida (-4.0%) and Georgia (-3.9%). At $2,950 per enrollee, Texas maintains spending discipline slightly above the national average of $2,851 but significantly below expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,368) and Ohio ($3,522). The state's managed care penetration of 93.3% substantially exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong commitment to coordinated care delivery models that have helped contain costs while serving a large, diverse population.

However, Texas faces persistent quality challenges that warrant immediate attention. With a quality score of 62/100, the state ranks 30th nationally and falls into the "moderate" tier—a concerning position for such a large program affecting millions of vulnerable residents. While avoiding the bottom tier occupied by other Southern states like Arkansas (54) and Mississippi (48), Texas lags behind quality leaders and even some peer non-expansion states. The dramatic -54.9% year-over-year expenditure decline suggests either significant policy changes or data reporting issues that require investigation. Key areas to monitor include whether the state can leverage its strong managed care infrastructure to drive quality improvements, how enrollment patterns evolve as pandemic-era protections fully unwind, and whether Texas will reconsider Medicaid expansion given the substantial federal funding available and coverage gaps affecting an estimated 1+ million uninsured adults.

alerts.title: Massive spending drops across all major states
alerts.description: All top 10 states show dramatic YoY spending decreases of 24-59%, indicating possible data reporting issues or major policy changes.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania per-enrollee spending 53% above national average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's $4,368 per-enrollee spending significantly exceeds the national average of $2,851, warranting cost structure review.

alerts.title: Five states show critically low quality scores
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all score below 55/100, well under the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Illinois shows high spending with below-average quality
alerts.description: Illinois combines relatively high per-enrollee spending ($2,708) with a quality score likely below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Florida achieves lowest per-enrollee costs among major states
alerts.description: Florida's $2,029 per-enrollee spending is 29% below the national average, representing potential cost efficiency model.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid expenditures plummeted 54.9% YoY - major cost event
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid expenditures dropped from approximately $26.7B to $12.0B year-over-year, a 54.9% decline that mirrors similar drops across major states. This represents a massive $14.7B reduction in program spending.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: This dramatic spending reduction likely signals the end of COVID-era enhanced federal funding or major policy changes that directly impact our contract scope, revenue, and service delivery requirements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag to HHSC leadership for immediate contract scope and revenue impact assessment

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score at 62/100 ranks #30 nationally - improvement opportunity
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scored 62/100 on quality measures, ranking 30th out of 50 states and falling in the 'moderate' tier, well below the national average of 65.6. High performers like MA scored 81/100.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Quality improvement represents a clear value proposition for Accenture services, and the gap between Texas and top performers suggests significant consulting opportunities around quality initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review quality improvement service offerings and benchmark against MA/MN programs

executiveInsights.title: Texas leads in managed care penetration at 93.3% vs 83.2% national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration covering 3.86M of 4.08M enrollees, significantly above the 83.2% national average. This represents one of the highest MC adoption rates nationally.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration indicates sophisticated systems requirements and potential for managed care optimization services, positioning Accenture for technology and analytics engagements.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care technology and analytics capabilities for expansion opportunities

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment decline of 2.2% is modest compared to other large states
executiveInsights.summary: Texas lost 2.2% of enrollees year-over-year while maintaining 4.08M total enrollees. This decline is smaller than CA (-5.6%), IL (-6.5%), and FL (-4.0%).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Texas's relatively stable enrollment suggests better program management and positions the state for continued federal investment, supporting contract stability and growth potential.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag Texas's enrollment stability as competitive advantage in future proposals

executiveInsights.title: Active Texas HHSC procurement portal signals upcoming opportunities
executiveInsights.summary: The Texas HHSC Procurement Opportunities portal is actively listed in external signals, indicating potential new RFPs or contract opportunities. Additional Texas ESBD portal also shows active procurement activity.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Active procurement signals suggest upcoming contract opportunities or renewals that Accenture should monitor closely for competitive positioning and business development.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review Texas HHSC procurement portals weekly for new RFP postings

riskOpportunity.label: Medicaid managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates high-stakes contract renewal opportunities as existing arrangements face recompetition.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish early stakeholder relationships and begin capability demonstrations ahead of formal RFP release.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality measure improvement gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks #30 nationally with a 62/100 quality score, creating urgent need for performance enhancement services.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop targeted quality improvement methodology focusing on Texas's specific performance gaps versus top-tier states.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal expenditure volatility
riskOpportunity.description: Texas experienced a dramatic -54.9% year-over-year expenditure decline, signaling potential budget instability and program changes.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Conduct immediate analysis of expenditure drivers and prepare flexible contract terms to accommodate budget fluctuations.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight intensification
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid portfolio oversight creates compliance risks but also opportunities for advisory services.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Proactively engage Texas HHSC on compliance readiness assessments and audit preparation services.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment stabilization challenge
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's -2.2% enrollment decline requires strategic intervention to maintain program sustainability and federal funding.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose enrollment retention and outreach optimization services to reverse negative enrollment trends.

riskOpportunity.label: Procurement portal modernization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas HHSC's active procurement opportunities portal indicates ongoing technology and service contract competitions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish systematic monitoring of Texas procurement portal and prepare rapid response capabilities for relevant RFPs.

riskOpportunity.label: Per-enrollee cost optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $2,950 per-enrollee cost sits above national average, creating pressure for cost management solutions.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive cost reduction proposals targeting administrative efficiency and care management optimization.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive displacement risk
riskOpportunity.description: Large-scale contract opportunities in Texas attract major competitors, threatening Accenture's market position.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Accelerate differentiation through proprietary IP development and exclusive partnership arrangements.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 4,083,939 Medicaid enrollees as of December 2025, ranking 3rd nationally.

pulseMetrics.label: 12-Mo Enrollment Delta
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas Medicaid enrollment declined 2.2% year-over-year, better than the national trend.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spends $2,950 per Medicaid enrollee annually, above the national average of $2,851.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 enrollees, well above the 83.2% national average.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks 30th of 50 states with a moderate quality score of 62, below the national average of 65.6.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active procurement portals are monitored: Texas HHSC opportunities and Electronic State Business Daily.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Ten active regulatory items identified from HHS OIG oversight and Federal Register filings.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific milestone data available in current dataset.
